location: San Pietro Island, Province Of Carbonia-Iglesias
description: This dive site off San Pietro Island features dramatic underwater cliffs and caves teeming with marine life. The rocky terrain drops steeply, creating a playground for groupers, moray eels, and colorful nudibranchs. Strong currents attract schools of barracuda and amberjack, making it ideal for drift diving. Visibility often exceeds 30 meters, revealing vibrant sponges and corals. The site's exposed position means conditions vary, rewarding experienced divers with thrilling encounters.

region: Europe, Italy, Sardinia, Province Of Carbonia-iglesias, San Pietro Island
ocean: Atlantic Ocean, Mediterranean Sea, Balearic Sea
location: San Pietro Island, Province Of Carbonia-Iglesias
description: Dive into crystal-clear Mediterranean waters off San Pietro Island, where dramatic underwater cliffs and rocky outcrops create a stunning seascape. Schools of barracuda and dentex patrol the blue, while colorful sponges and gorgonians cling to vertical walls. The site offers excellent visibility and mild currents, perfect for exploring caves and swim-throughs teeming with marine life. Keep an eye out for octopus hiding in crevices and groupers lurking near the drop-offs. Ideal for intermediate divers, this spot combines rich biodiversity with striking topography.

name: Beach Rock Di Spalmatore
region: Europe, Italy, Sardinia, Province Of Olbia-tempio
ocean: Atlantic Ocean, Mediterranean Sea, Tyrrhenian Sea
description: This dive site features a stunning underwater landscape with large granite boulders and rocky outcrops creating swim-throughs and caves. The clear Tyrrhenian Sea offers excellent visibility, often exceeding 20 meters. Colorful sponges and gorgonians cover the rocks while schools of damselfish, groupers, and moray eels inhabit the area. Depths range from 10 to 30 meters, suitable for various skill levels. The site's unique topography provides shelter for octopus and crustaceans. Currents are usually mild, making it ideal for relaxed exploration.

name: L'arco Delle Tacche Blanche
region: Europe, Italy, Sardinia, Province Of Carbonia-iglesias, San Pietro Island
ocean: Atlantic Ocean, Mediterranean Sea, Balearic Sea
location: San Pietro Island, Province Of Carbonia-Iglesias
description: This dive site off San Pietro Island features a stunning natural arch with white limestone walls, creating dramatic light effects. The arch drops to 30 meters, surrounded by vibrant gorgonians and sponges. Schools of barracuda and dentex patrol the area, while moray eels hide in crevices. Currents can be strong, making it ideal for drift diving. Visibility often exceeds 30 meters, revealing the site's impressive topography. Best suited for intermediate to advanced divers due to depth and conditions.

name: Scoglio Del Fico
region: Europe, Italy, Sardinia, Province Of Olbia-tempio, Isola Molara
ocean: Atlantic Ocean, Mediterranean Sea, Tyrrhenian Sea
description: Scoglio Del Fico is a stunning dive site off Isola Molara in Sardinia, known for its dramatic underwater cliffs and vibrant marine life. The rocky formations create swim-throughs and overhangs teeming with groupers, moray eels, and colorful nudibranchs. Clear waters offer excellent visibility, while mild currents attract schools of barracuda and amberjack. The site suits all skill levels, with depths ranging from shallow reefs to deeper drop-offs. Its unique topography and rich biodiversity make it a must-dive in the Tyrrhenian Sea.

ocean: Atlantic Ocean, Mediterranean Sea, Tyrrhenian Sea
description: A vibrant dive site near Serpentara Island, Sardinia, features dramatic granite formations and clear Tyrrhenian waters. Schools of barracuda and groupers patrol the rocky outcrops, while colorful sponges and corals cover the boulders. Depths range from 10 to 40 meters, with strong currents attracting pelagic life. The site offers excellent visibility and diverse marine encounters, from moray eels to occasional tuna. Ideal for advanced divers due to variable conditions.
